title: Quetzalia contracta
taxonomy: Quetzalia contracta is classified in the family Meliaceae. It belongs to the order Sapindales and the class Magnoliopsida. This classification places Quetzalia contracta within the larger group of flowering plants. The family Meliaceae is comprised of trees and shrubs commonly known as the mahogany family. The classification of Quetzalia contracta is a result of its shared characteristics with other species in this family.
uses: Quetzalia contracta is reportedly used in traditional medicine, primarily in the treatment of gastrointestinal issues and fever. intro: Quetzalia contracta, also known as Mexican Quetzalia, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Central America, characterized by its unique, yellowish-brown bark and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Quetzalia contracta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Quetzalia contracta is often made into teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in topical applications, such as creams and salves.
